target.title: Fix opencodego manual cookie usage on Linux CLI
target.body:
Fixes #1596

## Root cause
- The non-macOS CLI web-support gate only checked the effective source mode and provider source mode catalog.
- `opencodego` advertises `.web` for `.auto`, so Linux CLI exited before reaching `OpenCodeWebCookieSupport.resolveCookieHeader`.
- The manual cookie branch only normalizes the configured cookie header and does not require Safari, Chrome, WebKit, or browser cookie import.

## Fix strategy
- Keep the non-macOS web-support gate in place.
- Add a narrow `opencodego` exception only when `settings.opencodego.cookieSource == .manual`.
- Leave `opencodego` auto/browser cookie import gated on non-macOS.
- Add regression coverage proving `opencodego` manual is allowed, `opencodego` auto remains unsupported, and another web-backed provider is not accidentally allowed by manual cookie settings.

## Validation
- `env HOME=/Users/yuxinqiao/Documents/Codex/Codexbar-bugfix1/CodexBar-shallow.incomplete-20260617T1756/.test-home CLANG_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/Users/yuxinqiao/Documents/Codex/Codexbar-bugfix1/CodexBar-shallow.incomplete-20260617T1756/.clang-module-cache swift test --filter CLIEntryTests` - passed, 22 tests.
- `swiftformat Sources/CodexBarCLI/CLIUsageCommand.swift Tests/CodexBarTests/CLIEntryTests.swift` - completed, 0/2 files formatted.
- `swiftlint lint --no-cache Sources/CodexBarCLI/CLIUsageCommand.swift Tests/CodexBarTests/CLIEntryTests.swift` - passed, 0 violations.
